name: Rep. Steve Chabot
tweet: Let’s do our jobs as Members of this Committee and thoroughly investigate these allegations to get to the bottom of what happened.   Justice, history and the American people deserve at least that much.

name: Rep. Steve Chabot
tweet: . @RepJerryNadler we’ve served on this Committee and even as Chairman and Ranking Member alongside each other. If nothing more, I ask that you, at a minimum, take the time to bring fact witnesses to testify about the alleged quid pro quo.

name: Rep. Steve Chabot
tweet: What Democrats are doing today is a travesty in which they are setting the standard for future Administrations.

name: Rep. Steve Chabot
tweet: The majority really should hold themselves in contempt for conducting this one-sided biased impeachment investigation, and then attacking the White House for refusing to participate in such a patently unfair process.

name: Rep. Steve Chabot
tweet: You want to talk about an abuse of power?    What the House Democrats are doing here is a clear case of abusing their office for political gain.

name: Rep. Steve Chabot
tweet: When Congress disagrees with a particular assertion of executive privilege, the remedy is not impeachment. The remedy is to go to court, and let the third branch of government decide who is correct.

name: Rep. Steve Chabot
tweet: Executive privilege is a time-honored, constitutionally protected right of each and every Administration. And it has been asserted time and time again, by Administration after Administration.

name: Rep. Steve Chabot
tweet: Let me make this clear — asserting executive privilege is not, in any way, shape or form, obstruction.

name: Rep. Steve Chabot
tweet: The second Article of Impeachment is nearly as ridiculous as the first. An obstruction charge requires a concerted effort to interfere with or impede a Congressional investigation.

name: Rep. Steve Chabot
tweet: But that effort was abandoned because she knows, most Members of Congress know, and now the American people know, 𝐭𝐡𝐞𝐫𝐞 𝐬𝐢𝐦𝐩𝐥𝐲 𝐰𝐚𝐬𝐧’𝐭 𝐚 𝐜𝐫𝐢𝐦𝐞 𝐜𝐨𝐦𝐦𝐢𝐭𝐭𝐞𝐝 𝐡𝐞𝐫𝐞.

name: Rep. Steve Chabot
tweet: I guess we now know why Nancy Pelosi was focus-grouping bribery as a potential charge. Because she was desperately searching for a crime to justify this 𝐬𝐡𝐚𝐦 𝐢𝐦𝐩𝐞𝐚𝐜𝐡𝐦𝐞𝐧𝐭.

name: Rep. Steve Chabot
tweet: .@HouseDemocrats If that’s the best you’ve got, you wasted a whole lot of time and taxpayer dollars, all because the leftist, Democrat base hates this President.

name: Rep. Steve Chabot
tweet: Let me put it another way — the entire argument for impeachment is based on a charge that is not a crime (much less a high crime) and that has NEVER been approved by the House of Representatives in a Presidential Impeachment.

name: Rep. Steve Chabot
tweet: In the Nixon and Clinton impeachments, abuse of power was a tacked-on charge, far less important in those cases than the actual high crimes charged against both men. Here, it’s the main thrust of the House Democrats’ entire case.

name: Rep. Steve Chabot
tweet: There is another significant difference between the abuse of power charges against Nixon and Clinton, and those presented here.

name: Rep. Steve Chabot
tweet: Based on the actual facts of this case (as opposed to the hearsay and innuendo compiled by the Intelligence Committee), it doesn’t appear that an abuse of power even took place, and there certainly isn’t enough evidence to support an article of impeachment.

name: Rep. Steve Chabot
tweet: Fourth, despite all the efforts to establish evidence of a quid pro quo by House Democrats, Ukraine did nothing to assure the release of the aid. There was no public announcement of an investigation. In fact, it doesn’t appear that the investigation was even opened.

name: Rep. Steve Chabot
tweet: Third, Ukraine actually received the aid after the President was satisfied that Ukraine had taken meaningful steps to address corruption. Which, again, is an obligation required by law.

name: Rep. Steve Chabot
tweet: President Trump was well aware of that fact and quite skeptical of giving Ukraine foreign aid long before the now-infamous July 25 phone call.

name: Rep. Steve Chabot
tweet: Second, it’s a widely known fact that Ukraine is one of the most corrupt countries on the planet. It’s why Congress required the Administration to certify that the Ukrainian government had taken steps to clean up corruption before military aid could be provided to the country.

name: Rep. Steve Chabot
tweet: First, there was no quid pro quo.

name: Rep. Steve Chabot
tweet: In this case, the evidence provided is even less convincing. In fact, I’d argue it is 𝐰𝐨𝐞𝐟𝐮𝐥𝐥𝐲 𝐢𝐧𝐚𝐝𝐞𝐪𝐮𝐚𝐭𝐞.

name: Rep. Steve Chabot
tweet: In the interests of fairness, I believed that Bill Clinton had abused the power of his office, but we failed to convince our colleagues. The facts didn’t satisfy the higher burden of proof, and that particular charge was rejected by the full House.

name: Rep. Steve Chabot
tweet: What they don’t mention is that the House of Representatives has NEVER adopted abuse of power as a charge in a Presidential Impeachment.

name: Rep. Steve Chabot
tweet: In their newly authored memo on constitutional grounds for impeachment, the majority on this Committee goes to great lengths to explain why abuse of power is an impeachable offense, specifically mentioning it was one of the charges against both Richard Nixon and Bill Clinton.

name: Rep. Steve Chabot
tweet: Impeachment is reserved for instances of “Treason, Bribery and other high Crimes and Misdemeanors.” It’s not “Treason, Bribery, other high Crimes and Misdemeanors” OR whatever else Nancy Pelosi and Adam Schiff deem impeachable.

name: Rep. Steve Chabot
tweet: The Constitution is pretty clear on what constitutes an impeachable offense.

name: Rep. Steve Chabot
tweet: This is a sad day in American history, Mr. Chairman.   The folks in the liberal media might be cheering you on, but I highly doubt that either history, or the American people, will judge you so leniently.

name: Rep. Steve Chabot
tweet: To satisfy their base’s extreme hatred of President Trump, House Democrats have taken a blowtorch to House rules, the rule of law, and most frighteningly, to the Bill of Rights.

name: Rep. Steve Chabot
tweet: If George Orwell had written the script, no one would’ve believed it.  People would say that it’s ridiculously implausible. And yet here we are.

name: Rep. Steve Chabot
tweet: Tomorrow, this committee will hold a vote to impeach a president, without having heard from a single fact witness, and without allowing the minority party the ability to call any witnesses or present any defense.   What a travesty of justice!

name: Rep. Steve Chabot
tweet: The Chairman of this committee, and the majority have seen fit to abuse this committee’s rules, and ignore the rights of the minority with impunity.

name: Rep. Steve Chabot
tweet: At the same time, Republicans on this committee were denied their absolute right to have a minority hearing day, which is guaranteed by this committee’s own rules.

name: Rep. Steve Chabot
tweet: On Monday, during our second hearing, w/o any fact witnesses, we had the bizarre situation in which rather than Members questioning witnesses, we had staff questioning staff, & even had a staff witness get up from the witness stand & begin questioning another staff witness.

name: Rep. Steve Chabot
tweet: On Wednesday, this Committee, the committee actually charged with handling impeachment, held the first of two hearings, in which we heard from EXACTLY ZERO fact witnesses.

name: Rep. Steve Chabot
tweet: After the farce in the Intelligence Committee, we had dumped in our laps a report, recommending impeachment, with no time to adequately consider or review the materials.

name: Rep. Steve Chabot
tweet: We are witnessing an inexplicable rush to impeach a President who is disliked, no loathed, by most of my Democratic colleagues, and by their supporters, and as a result of that loathing, they have seen fit to abandon all basic tenets of fairness, due process, and justice.

name: Rep. Steve Chabot
tweet: We are witnessing, I believe, the most tragic mockery of justice, in the history of this nation.

name: Rep. Steve Chabot
tweet: I have to say that the last few weeks have been just about the most incredible, and by that I mean most unbelievable, few weeks in my 23 years of service in this Congress.
